input.counterSource: No credible counter-source found. The older grid-template-rows: masonry syntax that might have contradicted the claim was formally abandoned at the January 31, 2025 CSSWG meeting. All current 2025-2026 sources agree on display: grid-lanes.
input.evidence: The MDN page at the cited URL confirms: "Level 3 of the CSS grid layout specification defines masonry layout (also known as grid-lanes layout), which is accessed via the display values grid-lanes and inline-grid-lanes." The supporting quote directly backs every element of the claim — the spec level (Level 3), the grid-lanes model name, and the use of display: grid-lanes rather than grid-template-rows: masonry. Multiple corroborating sources (CSS-Tricks "Masonry Layout is Now grid-lanes", WebKit blog "Introducing CSS Grid Lanes", Chrome Developers blog) confirm the old grid-template-rows: masonry syntax (Firefox-only, now dropped by CSSWG) was superseded by grid-lanes after a January 2025 CSSWG resolution. The claim accurately represents the current spec. The only minor looseness is calling it "under the grid-lanes model" when the spec calls it "grid-lanes layout", but this is not an overreach — the meaning is the same. Source quality is high (MDN primary docs, browser-vendor blogs). The claim is current as of 2026.
